data.title: Mobilicom to replace ADSs with ordinary shares on Nasdaq from Dec. 8
data.description:
• Mobilicom (MOB) on Thursday said it will transition its American Depositary Shares to a direct listing of its ordinary shares on the Nasdaq Capital Market, effective at the market open on December 8.
  • The ADR program will terminate after market close on December 5, alongside a 1-for-275 reverse share split.
  • Each ADS will be cancelled and converted into one ordinary share, with no action required by holders.
  • Mobilicom said the move is intended to simplify its structure, cut administrative costs and improve U.S. investor access, including potential eligibility for indices and ETFs that exclude ADRs.
  • MOB -0.0% after hours to $7.43.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Darling Ingredients announces agreement to sell about $60M in production tax credits
data.description:
• Darling Ingredients (DAR) on Thursday announced an agreement to sell approximately $60 million of production tax credits to a corporate buyer.
  • These credits were generated under the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) by the company’s Diamond Green Diesel joint venture. The proceeds of the sale are scheduled to be received by Dec. 31, 2025, upon satisfaction of certain funding conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PTC appoints new CFO
data.description:
• PTC (PTC) named Jennifer DiRico as executive vice president and chief financial officer, effective January 1, 2026.
  • DiRico succeeds outgoing CFO Kristian Talvitie, who will remain in the role through December 31, 2025.
  • DiRico brings experience from Commvault, where she currently serves as CFO.
  • PTC reaffirmed its full fiscal year 2026 and Q1 guidance previously issued on November 5, 2025.

data.title: Ulta raises outlook after strong Q3 while higher debt tempers the glow
data.description:
Ulta Beauty (ULTA) crushed Wall Street’s expectations for the third quarter with a solid top- and bottom-line beat and comparable store sales that were nearly double expectations, driving shares higher in after-hours trading.

“Exciting assortment newness, improved in-store and digital experiences, and bold marketing efforts are resonating with our guests and drove strong sales results, market share gains, and growth across all categories and channels, with notable strength in ecommerce,” said Ulta CEO Kecia Steelman.

To reflect the strong third quarter and to incorporate the company’s expectation for a successful holiday season, Ulta Beauty (ULTA) raised its full year guidance again. The company now sees sales to be ~$12.3B, up from prior guidance of $12.0B to $12.1B, and better than $12.06B estimates, with comparable store sales expected to increase 4.4% to 4.7% from +2.5% to +3.5%, previously.

Profitability is also expected to be better than initially expected with EPS now seen between $25.20 and $25.50 per share from $23.85 and $24.30 per share, above the $24.57 estimate. Operating margin guidance was raised to a range of 12.3% to 12.4% from 11.9% to 12.0%.

For the reported quarter, increased traffic, new stores, and the acquisition of Space NK drove sales up 13% to a better-than-expected $2.9B. Comparable store sales jumped 6.3% from 0.6% a year ago and beat 3.41% estimates.

On the bottom line, Ulta (ULTA) earned an adjusted profit of $5.14 per share, unchanged from the same quarter last year, but $0.53 better than anticipated. And reflecting lower inventory shrink and a higher margin on merchandise, gross profit margin increased 70 basis points to 40.4%.

The balance sheet, however, took a hit. To support share repurchases, capital expenditures, and “ongoing capital allocation priorities,” the company fell further into debt with short-term debt swelling nearly 3x to $551.7M. During the quarter, Ulta (ULTA) repurchased 426,914 shares at a cost of $224.7M, opened 28 new stores, and remodeled 15 existing stores.

data.title: USA Rare Earth rips higher after supply deal with Compass Diversified, Solvay
data.description:
USA Rare Earth (USAR) closed +24.6% on Thursday after announcing earlier that its Less Common Metals subsidiary signed an agreement to supply European chemical company Solvay (OTCPK:SVYSF) (OTCPK:SLVYY) and Compass Diversified (CODI) subsidiary Arnold Magnetic Technologies with high-quality rare earth materials for Arnold's production of advanced permanent magnets; financial details were not provided.

The agreement comes less than a month after USA Rare Earth (USAR) closed its acquisition of Less Common Metals for $100 million, citing LCI's unique position as the only proven ex-China producer of both light and heavy rare earth permanent magnet metals and alloys at scale at its 67,000 sq ft production facility in the U.K.

"This collaboration exemplifies the type of industrial partnerships that are rebuilding strength and resilience across the rare-earth ecosystem outside of China," USA Rare Earth (USAR) CEO Barbara Humpton said.

USA Rare Earth (USAR) shares extended gains through the afternoon as a Trump administration official said the U.S. government plans to take more equity stakes in critical minerals companies.

data.title: The New York Times takes Pentagon to court over new press policy
data.description:
The New York Times (NYT) said Thursday it is suing the Pentagon after it implemented a new policy on U.S. military reporting, which the newspaper alleges violates its journalists' First and Fifth Amendment rights.

The newspaper claimed its journalists are at risk of losing their "Pentagon Facilities Alternate Credential" if they publish anything that has not been approved by the government officials under the new policy.

The 41-page lawsuit alleges that the Pentagon's new policy will deprive the public of vital information about the American military and its leadership, and the policy seeks to restrict journalists' ability "to ask questions of government employees and gather information to report stories that take the public beyond official pronouncements."

"The Pentagon has made clear that lawful, routine newsgathering techniques—asking questions of government employees and interviewing them for stories—whether on or off Pentagon grounds could, in the Department's view,  "constitute a solicitation that could lead to revocation" of their PFACs," the lawsuit said.

The lawsuit notably includes War Secretary Pete Hegseth and Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell as defendants and NYT's U.S. intelligence agencies reporter Julian Barnes as a plaintiff. The case is The New York Times Company v. Department of Defense, Civil Case No. 25-4218, filed in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia.

Earlier this year, major news outlets, including NYT, Reuters (NASDAQ:TRI), The Wall Street Journal (NASDAQ:NWS) (NASDAQ:NWSA), Associated Press, Bloomberg News, The Washington Post, CNN (NASDAQ:WBD), Fox News (NASDAQ:FOX) (NASDAQ:FOXA), CBS (NASDAQ:PSKY), NBC (NASDAQ:CMCSA), ABC (NYSE:DIS), NPR, Axios, Politico, The Guardian, The Atlantic, and Newsmax (NYSE:NMAX), objected to the Pentagon's press policy.

News outlets argued that the Pentagon's new policy threatened to criminalize national security reporting and restrict the gathering of even unclassified information not pre-approved for release.

"The journalism produced by The Times and other outlets who refused to sign this policy provides critical information to the American public about the actions the U.S. military undertakes in their name and financial expense, and serves members of the military by reporting on matters of health, safety, housing, and foreign deployments," according to the official statement by The New York Times.

So far, only one major national news outlet, One America News Network, has publicly signed the new press policy.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: KNOT Offshore Partners reports Q3 results
data.description:
• KNOT Offshore Partners press release (KNOP): Q3 Revenue of $96.9M.
  • Depreciation of $30.9 million in Q3 2025 ($29.4 million in Q2 2025).
  • General and administrative expenses of $1.5 million in Q3 2025 ($1.6 million in Q2 2025).
  • Operating income consequently of $30.7 million in Q3 2025 ($22.2 million in Q2 2025).
  • Interest expense of $16.5 million in Q3 2025 ($15.3 million in Q2 2025).
  • Realized (i.e. cash) gain on derivative instruments of $2.2 million in Q3 2025 (gain of $2.5 million in Q2 2025), and unrealized (i.e. non-cash) loss of $1.8 million in Q3 2025 (unrealized loss of $2.9 million in Q2 2025). Together, there was a realized and unrealized gain on derivative instruments of $0.4 million in Q3 2025 (loss of $0.4 million in Q2 2025).
  • Net income consequently of $15.1 million in Q3 2025 ($6.8 million in Q2 2025).

  • As of September 30, 2025, the Partnership had $125.2 million in available liquidity, which was comprised of cash and cash equivalents of $77.2 million and $48.0 million of capacity under its revolving credit facilities. The Partnership’s revolving credit facilities mature in August 2027 and November 2027 respectively.

data.title: Commvault CFO Jen DiRico to depart; company begins search for successor
data.description:
• Commvault (CVLT) on Thursday said that Jen DiRico, Chief Financial Officer, will be departing the company at the end of the calendar year to pursue another opportunity.
  • CEO Sanjay Mirchandani will oversee an interim Office of the CFO, which includes VP of Finance Kevin White and Chief Accounting Officer Danielle Abrahamsen, while a search for a new finance chief is underway.
  • The company notes there are no changes to its outlook as previously disclosed in its second quarter earnings results on October 28, 2025.
  • Commvault expects to report its third quarter 2026 results at the end of January 2026.
  • CVLT -0.0% after hours to $121.34.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SoFi launches $1.5B public offering of common stock
data.description:
• SoFi Technologies (SOFI) announced an underwritten public offering of $1.5B in common stock.
  • The company plans to grant Goldman Sachs, the underwriter, a 30-day option to purchase up to 15% additional shares.
  • All shares in the offering will be sold by SoFi, with proceeds intended for general corporate purposes including strengthening capital, improving capital management flexibility, and funding growth opportunities.
  • The offering will be conducted under SoFi’s automatic shelf registration statement, effective since July 29, 2025.
  • SOFI shares down 4.7% post-market on Thursday.
